title: ADA Standards of Care in Diabetes 2025
summary: Metformin remains a common first-line therapy when tolerated and renal function is adequate. GLP-1 receptor agonists and SGLT2 inhibitors are prioritized for cardiometabolic risk reduction.
risk: renal thresholds and GI adverse effects

title: EASD/ADA Consensus Update on Hyperglycemia Management
summary: Treatment should be personalized by comorbidity, baseline HbA1c, and patient adherence patterns. Combination therapy can improve control when baseline HbA1c is high.

title: Semaglutide and Glycemic Outcomes in Type 2 Diabetes
summary: Semaglutide improves HbA1c and body weight in adults with type 2 diabetes, with gastrointestinal adverse events as common side effects.

title: SGLT2 inhibitors and cardio-renal outcomes
summary: SGLT2 inhibitors can reduce heart failure hospitalization and slow renal decline in selected populations.

title: Basal insulin intensification patterns in uncontrolled T2D
summary: Basal insulin can rapidly improve glycemia for high baseline HbA1c, but hypoglycemia risk rises when adherence and monitoring are poor.

title: Lifestyle intervention and long-term glycemic response
summary: Structured nutrition and exercise interventions improve glycemic trajectories and medication response durability.
